articles.description: Chip-testing equipment maker Teradyne on Tuesday forecast a sequential decline ‌in second-quarter revenue and adjusted profit, sending ‌its shares down more than 8% in extended trading. The ​forecast comes as Teradyne's first-quarter revenue jumped 87% from a year earlier, driven by strong demand for its equipment used to test high-performance ‌semiconductors for ⁠the artificial intelligence market. • Teradyne sees second-quarter revenue in the range of $1.15 ⁠billion to $1.25 billion, the midpoint of which is lower than its first-quarter revenue of $1.28 billion.
